title: redact_token_query_param does not redact 'TOKEN=' (uppercase) or URL-encoded '%74oken='
description: The redact_token_query_param middleware only checks for lowercase 'token=' prefix. If a browser constructs a query string with Token=, TOKEN=, or URL-encoded %74oken= (which would not reach this logic since axum decodes query strings before routing), the token value would appear in trace logs unredacted. HTTP specs and browsers always send lowercase, so in practice this is unlikely to fire for ?token= — but the mismatch between the check (lowercase token=) and the actual header format creates a maintenance trap: if the middleware is extended to handle other params, the pattern needs consistent case handling. The comment 'token= is found' in the code also says 'token ' (with space) which would never appear in a query string but is in the check.
